title: Arjun and the Lost Baby Bird
synopsis: When young Arjun finds a scared baby bird that has fallen from its nest, he feels worried but becomes brave to help his new feathered friend find its way back home to its mama.

pages.text: Arjun was playing in his backyard when he heard a tiny sound. "Peep! Peep!" He looked down and saw a small yellow baby bird on the grass. The little bird looked scared and lost. Arjun felt worried. "Oh no, little bird! Where is your mama?" he said softly.

illustration.description: A young South Asian boy with dark hair and kind brown eyes kneeling on green grass, looking down at a small fluffy yellow chick. Arjun wears a bright red t-shirt and blue shorts, his expression showing gentle concern. The backyard has colorful flowers and a wooden fence. The baby bird sits on the textured grass looking up with big round eyes, appearing small and vulnerable.
illustration.alt: Arjun kneeling in the grass looking at a small yellow baby bird

pages.text: The baby bird tried to flap its tiny wings, but it couldn't fly yet. It looked up at Arjun with big, round eyes. Arjun's grandma Nani came outside and saw them together. "The baby must have fallen from its nest," she said kindly. "What should we do, Nani?" Arjun asked. He wanted to help, but he felt a little scared too.

illustration.description: Arjun sitting cross-legged on the grass next to the baby bird, with his grandmother standing nearby. Nani is a warm-looking older woman with gray hair in a colorful sari, smiling gently. Above them, a large tree with green leaves shows a bird's nest high in the branches. The composition shows the height difference between the ground and the nest, emphasizing the bird's journey.
illustration.alt: Arjun, his grandmother, and the baby bird looking up at a tall tree with a nest

pages.text: "We need to be brave and help this little one," Nani said. She helped Arjun gently pick up the baby bird. It felt so soft and warm in his hands. Arjun took a deep breath. Even though he felt nervous, he wanted to be brave for the baby bird. "Don't worry, little friend. We'll help you get home," he whispered. The bird stopped peeping and looked at him with trust.

illustration.description: Arjun carefully holding the fluffy yellow baby bird in his cupped hands, his face showing determination mixed with gentle care. Nani stands beside him with an encouraging smile, her hand on his shoulder. The setting sun creates warm golden light around them, and colorful flowers bloom in the background. The baby bird looks calm and safe in Arjun's protective hands.
illustration.alt: Arjun holding the baby bird gently while his grandmother encourages him

pages.text: Nani brought a small ladder, and together they climbed up to the nest. Arjun's heart was beating fast, but he felt brave. He carefully placed the baby bird back in its cozy nest. "Mama! Mama!" the baby bird chirped happily. Soon, a big blue bird flew down - it was the mama bird! She looked so happy to see her baby. Arjun felt proud and joyful. "We did it!" he said with a big smile.

illustration.description: A joyful scene showing Arjun on a ladder placing the baby bird into a woven nest in the tree branches, while a beautiful blue mother bird approaches with wings spread wide. Both birds are chirping happily, with musical notes floating around them. Nani holds the ladder steady below, and everyone has expressions of happiness and relief. The tree leaves frame the scene in bright greens.
illustration.alt: Happy reunion as Arjun returns the baby bird to its nest and mama

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Arjun feel when he first saw the baby bird?
questions.explanation: Arjun felt worried when he saw the scared and lost baby bird on the grass.

questions.question: Who helped Arjun return the baby bird to its nest?
questions.explanation: Arjun's Nani (grandmother) helped him by bringing a ladder and supporting him.

questions.question: How did Arjun feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Arjun felt proud and joyful because he was brave and helped the baby bird get back to its mama.

questions.question: What did the baby bird do when it saw its mama?
questions.explanation: The baby bird chirped 'Mama! Mama!' happily when it was reunited with its mother.

pages.text: Arjun found a _____ baby bird on the grass. The little bird looked scared and _____.
Arjun wanted to be _____ and help the baby bird get back to its _____.
When the _____ bird returned, everyone felt _____ and joyful.
